text: We switched from Semrush to BrightEdge last year. Semrush was easier to get started with, but BrightEdge's data quality is far better for enterprise accounts.
ChatGPT has completely changed how our team does research. We use it every day and it saves hours of work.

We evaluated Salesforce and HubSpot for our CRM rollout. Salesforce had more features but was too expensive and the implementation took months. HubSpot was easier to adopt and the support was great, though it lacked some advanced reporting.
AWS is the backbone of our infrastructure. It is reliable and the ecosystem is unmatched, but the billing is a nightmare to understand.
Notion is beautiful and the templates are lovely. But Notion keeps crashing on large databases, sync is unreliable, and after the third data loss we are canceling our subscription.
For backlink analysis Ahrefs is simply better than Semrush right now, and Moz feels dated in comparison.
I ate an apple while setting up my new MacBook. Apple's setup flow is genuinely seamless.
note: the fruit must not be extracted; MacBook neutral/positive both defensible

text: The weather was great and we hiked all afternoon before cooking dinner at the campsite.
Slack is not bad at all - honestly it has been rock solid for our remote team.
We moved most workloads to Azure last quarter and our costs dropped noticeably.
GCP's documentation is a mess compared to AWS, we lost two days to it.
note: AWS positive via favorable comparison; neutral also arguable

text: Twitter, now X, has lost most of the features I actually loved using.

text: The iPhone's camera is unbelievable this year, easily the best reason to upgrade.

Oh great, another Jira update that somehow makes every page load even slower.

text: HubSpot onboarding was rough and took weeks. Still, HubSpot's automation now saves us ten hours a week and support has been stellar. HubSpot ended up being the right call.

[Nationwide Crane Training](https://www.nationwidecranetraining.com/product/online-qualified-rigging-signalperson-training/) provides live online Qualified Rigger and Signalperson webinars that meet OSHA training requirements.
note: OSHA is a regulator, not a commercial entity

text: After two outages in a month we migrated off DigitalOcean to Hetzner and honestly have not looked back.
Stripe processes payments for millions of businesses across more than forty countries.
Delta canceled my flight twice this month and rebooking support was useless.
